segments.text: This study proposes a system that supports audio-only remote communication by conveying the listener's reactions to the speaker through backchannel audio.
This is the eyeglass-shaped device we developed for the system.
It has an IMU sensor on the left side of the frame, which detects the listener's head gestures, such as nodding or shaking.
When a gesture is detected, the system sends a reaction signal to the speaker's side by playing an appropriate pre-recorded backchannel audio.
The selection of the backchannel audio is handled by an AI agent.
It considers the type of head gesture, its strength, and the conversational context.
You can experience how the system works by acting as a listener.
Would you like to try it?
デバイスを装着してもらう
Then you will hear an audio clip, which you can think of as the speaker's voice.
While listening, please nod or shake your head freely to react to the speaker.
In a real communication setting, the backchannel audio you just heard would be played to the speaker.
In this way, the system can convey the listener's nonverbal cues to the speaker and improve the quality of audio-only remote communication.
